competitor_patterns: Top-ranking articles (Outdoorgearlab, Bikerumor) are 15,000–25,000 words — 3–6x longer than our longest posts (4,639 words), with exhaustive multi-product testing sections, not just a buyer's guide framing

All top-3 pages use comparison tables AND per-product review sections with individual H2s per model (e.g., 'Julbo Density', 'Oakley Sutro Lite') — giving Google strong entity signals for each brand mentioned

Top competitors lead with 'tested by us' credibility signals in both H1 and meta description ('We tested 18 of the best...', 'Tested & Ranked') — Velluto posts use similar language but competitor sites have years of domain authority behind those claims
FAQ sections are universal across all top-3 pages, AND competitor pages include structured comparison tables — our FAQ schema coverage is only 33%, leaving rich-result slots on the table

Top pages target multiple sub-intent H2s simultaneously (MTB, Road, Value, Budget, Performance) within one URL, capturing a wide keyword cluster under a single authoritative page rather than splitting into thin separate posts
our_gaps: Word count gap is critical: our best posts are ~4,600 words vs competitor average of 15,000–25,000 words for the same head keywords — Google interprets depth as authority for 'best cycling glasses' cluster

We have 20+ posts under 1,400 words targeting competitive keywords — these thin pages likely dilute crawl budget and fail to satisfy search intent depth; they need either consolidation or significant expansion
FAQ schema is missing on 27 of 41 posts (67%) — a direct missed opportunity for rich results on queries where we already rank positions 4–9
Comparison tables are absent on 22 posts — top competitors use tables on every major roundup, and Google's AI Overviews frequently pull structured comparison data directly from tables

We have no posts explicitly targeting sub-intents like 'best MTB sunglasses 2026', 'best gravel cycling glasses 2026', or 'best budget cycling glasses under €100' — all H2 categories that top competitors use to capture long-tail cluster traffic within their pillar pages
keyword_opportunities: best cycling sunglasses for MTB 2026 — Outdoorgearlab ranks #1 with a dedicated H2 section; we have zero MTB-specific coverage despite having a relevant product

interchangeable lens cycling glasses 2026 — Alibaba's #1 ranking is a 404 error, meaning there is a clear gap for a properly optimised page on this exact query where we have a direct product advantage
best cycling glasses for all conditions — implied by photochromic and weather-related queries we already target but haven't consolidated into one authoritative 'all conditions' pillar
cycling glasses with anti-fog system 2026 — we have two thin posts on this topic (1,032 and 1,153 words) but no authoritative long-form piece despite anti-fog being a core StradaPro differentiator

lightweight cycling glasses 25g — ultra-specific weight claim (our USP) that no competitor appears to own; a targeted post around 'lightest cycling glasses 2026' could capture a niche with high purchase intent
next_post_guidelines: Target the 'oakley alternatives' cluster (239 + 66 + 57 + 51 impressions, CTR 1.5–2.9%) with a single consolidated long-form post of 8,000–12,000 words that includes individual H2 review sections for each alternative brand, a comparison table, and FAQ schema — do not create another thin standalone post

For 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (228 impressions, pos 8.1) and 'best cycling glasses 2026' (127 impressions, pos 7.2), the next post must target 10,000+ words with sub-sections covering Road, Gravel, MTB, Budget, and Premium tiers explicitly as H2s — mirror Outdoorgearlab's structure but lead with Velluto StradaPro in each relevant category

Every post published from today must include FAQ schema markup — prioritise retrofitting the 20 highest-impression posts without it (starting with 'best-cycling-glasses-for-tour-de-france-conditions-2026' at 1,264 words + table, and 'interchangeable-lens-cycling-glasses-2026-best-systems-tested' at 1,712 words + table)

Lead every meta description and H1 with a testable claim number: '25g', '30-day trial', 'two lenses', or a specific test result — mirror Outdoorgearlab and Bikerumor's 'We tested X models' credibility framing to improve CTR from current 1.5–3.9% range

Add a comparison table to all posts currently missing one, starting with the highest-impression pages: 'best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked' (3,924 words, no table) and 'are-expensive-cycling-glasses-worth-it-honest-answer' (3,806 words, no table) — these are your biggest structural quick wins

For the 'veluto' misspelling query (242 impressions, pos 5.5, CTR 0.8%), add a clearly visible brand name clarification in the meta title and first paragraph of the main brand/homepage content — 'Velluto (not Veluto)' — to capture navigational intent leaking to competitors

Structure all new comparison posts with individual H2 per competitor brand reviewed (e.g., 'Oakley Sutro Lite Sweep', 'Roka SR-1X', 'SunGod Velans') then a final H2 for StradaPro — this entity-level structure is what top competitors use and what Google's AI Overview pulls from
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema to all 27 posts currently missing it — prioritise by impressions; this alone could unlock rich result snippets for queries where we already hold positions 4–9 and is a one-hour implementation task per post

Add a comparison table to the 22 posts missing one — minimum 5-column table (Product / Weight / Lens System / Price / Trial Policy) with Velluto StradaPro in row 1; tables are the single most-cited structural element in top-3 competitor pages and are directly used in AI Overview responses

Consolidate the 8 posts under 1,100 words targeting overlapping anti-fog, budget, and fit keywords into 2–3 expanded 3,000+ word posts with proper internal linking — thin content fragmentation is hurting crawl efficiency and splitting ranking signals that should concentrate on fewer, stronger URLs
meta_title_fixes: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Store | Why: Captures misspelling navigational intent, corrects brand name, signals official source to increase trust-CTR from 0.8%

oakley alternatives → New title: 7 Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026 | Why: Number-led titles consistently outperform generic ones; '7' creates specificity and expectation that lifts CTR from 2.9%

oakley sunglasses alternative → New title: Best Oakley Sunglasses Alternative: 25g, €X Less | Why: Leads with StradaPro's weight USP and price advantage — specific claims lift CTR from 1.5% by targeting purchase-intent searchers

best oakley alternatives → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives 2026 — Tested & Ranked | Why: Adds year for freshness and 'Tested & Ranked' mirrors the top-CTR format used by Outdoorgearlab and Bikerumor, lifting from 1.7%

oakley alternative → New title: Oakley Alternative Cycling Glasses 2026 | Velluto | Why: Adds year signal and brand anchor — short tail 'oakley alternative' needs year + brand to differentiate from generic results, CTR from 1.8%

oakley radar alternative → New title: Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: Lighter & Cheaper? | Why: Question format + specific product reference + year drives higher CTR from 2.0% by matching comparison-intent searchers

best cycling sunglasses 2026 (pos 8.1) → Topic: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 — Tested Across Road, Gravel & MTB | Add: Expand existing post to 10,000+ words with separate H2 sections for Road, Gravel, MTB, Budget Under €100, Premium Over €200 — directly mirrors the Outdoorgearlab structure ranking #1 at 16,540 words

best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 7.2) → Topic: Consolidate with 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' post or create a 10,000-word definitive guide | Add: Add 'Quick Picks' jump table at top (mirrors Outdoorgearlab), per-model H2 review sections, a 'How We Tested' section, and FAQ schema — this is the single highest-ROI content expansion available given 127 impressions at pos 7.2

best cycling glasses (pos 8.9) → Topic: Best Cycling Glasses — Evergreen Guide (Updated 2026) | Add: Target this as a separate evergreen URL without year in slug, add 'Last updated July 2026' schema, include a buyer's decision tree section and FAQ schema — year-agnostic query needs an evergreen page not a dated one

best cycling sunglasses (pos 7.5) → Topic: Internal link consolidation | Add: Ensure the 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' post (3,294 words, table+FAQ schema) receives internal links from all 8 Oakley-alternative posts using anchor text 'best cycling sunglasses' — link consolidation alone can move this from 7.5 to top 5
geo_angles: Structure FAQ answers as direct single-sentence responses followed by supporting detail — AI Overviews and Perplexity pull the first complete sentence after a question header; answers like 'The Velluto StradaPro is the lightest cycling glass at 25g with interchangeable lenses and a 30-day trial' are structured for AI extraction

Create a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro vs [Brand]' comparison hub page with a summary table comparing weight, lens system, trial policy, and price across 6–8 competitors — AI answer engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity) frequently cite structured comparison pages when answering 'what is the best alternative to X' queries

Add a clearly marked 'Expert Verdict' or 'Bottom Line' section to every post using consistent formatting (bold label, 2–3 sentences) — Google AI Overviews specifically extract labelled verdict sections, and this pattern appears in both Outdoorgearlab and Bikerumor top-ranking content
comparison_post_angles: OUTDOORGEARLAB: Authority arbitrage — headline: 'We Tested the Same Glasses as OutdoorGearLab: Here's What They Missed About Weight' | key differentiator: Outdoorgearlab tests rarely highlight sub-30g frames; lead with StradaPro's 25g claim and how frame weight affects 4+ hour ride fatigue | Velluto advantage: lightest frame in class + 30-day real-ride trial vs lab testing

BIKERUMOR: Value depth — headline: 'Bikerumor Ranked These Glasses at €200+. We Built Something Better for Less.' | key differentiator: Bikerumor's top picks (Julbo Density, Oakley Sutro Lite) all exceed €150; StradaPro delivers comparable optical quality at lower price with Italian design | Velluto advantage: price-to-performance ratio + interchangeable lens system included vs paid upgrades

CYCLIST.CO.UK: Road-specific authority — headline: 'Best Road Cycling Glasses 2026: Why the Lightest Frame Wins on Long Climbs' | key differentiator: Cyclist.co.uk focuses on Oakley Sphaera as do-it-all; StradaPro is purpose-built for road with VellutoVisione high-contrast lens for variable light | Velluto advantage: road-specific lens optimisation + adjustable nose pads for helmet compatibility on long road rides
